home.og_description: Groups of writers publish together on FreedInk. Individual authorship is hidden by zero-knowledge cryptography.
home.subhead: This is a blogging platform that is a bit different than others. We aim to construct blogs shaped around like minds who want to speak more freely, but with some weight behind it.

blog.not_found_hint: Maybe the blog was deleted or there was a mistype?

comments.heading: Comments
comments.anonymous_hint: Comments are anonymous: posting proves you're a member of this blog without revealing which one.
comments.sign_in_to_comment_suffix: to comment as a blog member.

auth.invite_banner_signed_in: Hold on while we add you to the blog…
auth.invite_banner_signed_out: Sign in or create an account below — we'll add you to the blog right after.
auth.skip_to_dashboard: Skip and go to your dashboard

identity.intro: Welcome, {username}. Freed.Ink generates a Semaphore identity that lets you post anonymously inside blogs you're a member of. The secret part of that identity is encrypted with the password you pick below and stored in our database.
identity.forgot_warning: If you forget it, you'll have to rotate to a new identity (past posts stay attributed to the old one and remain verifiable).
identity.already_have: You already have an active identity. Use Settings → Rotate identity to change it.
identity.error_min_length: password must be at least 12 characters

admin.no_owned: You don't own any blogs yet.
admin.no_editing: You aren't an editor on any blogs yet.
admin.no_reviewing: You aren't a reviewer on any blogs yet.
admin.no_authoring: You aren't an author on any blogs yet.

settings.heading: Settings
settings.rotate_identity_blurb: Generates a new Semaphore identity, revokes the active one, and refreshes the membership snapshot of every blog you belong to. Past proofs (votes, comments, posts) keep verifying against their original snapshot.
settings.revoke_confirm: Revoke this session? Any device using it will be signed out.
settings.data_rights_blurb_suffix: for full details on what these do (and what they don't — anonymous posts stay).
settings.deletion_explanation: This permanently deletes your account, sessions, passkeys, wallets, identity blobs, and blog memberships. Anonymous posts and comments remain (they are not linked to your account in our database).
settings.error_confirm_username: confirmation must equal your username exactly
settings.identity_rotated: identity rotated; snapshots updated for every blog you belong to
settings.rotate_password_error: pick a password ≥12 chars and confirm it
settings.rotate_confirm_prompt: Rotate identity? Past posts stay attributed to the old identity.
